ghost.title: The Ghost of Times
ghost.motto: Written by those who lived it. Reporting for those who do.
ghost.blurb: A daily paper in which writers across the centuries — Carlyle, Chesterton, Hemingway, Whitman, Jung and the rest — each file one op-ed on the news of the day.

fingerprint.title: The Fingerprint
fingerprint.kicker: The global programmatic & Advanced TV market paper
fingerprint.motto: All the news that's fit to Fingerprint
fingerprint.blurb: An independent daily wire on the global programmatic and Advanced TV (CTV) market — platforms, performance, partnerships, competition, regulation, political-ad spend, and a five-market global desk, with closing op-eds in the voices of McLuhan and Ogilvy.

collections.title: Faith & Doubt
collections.essay: Belief at the edge of reason. The historical Jesus and his forerunner; Jung's God-image transformed in Answer to Job; the Calvinist substrate that outlived its creed; and phenomenology's reach toward the holy. Five corpora, read in sequence, become one argument about what survives when faith meets the modern mind.

collections.title: The Medium & the Message
collections.essay: How media remakes the people who make it. McLuhan's probes — the medium as the message — carried forward into the IP address layer that now identifies the watcher. From the global village to the machine that reads your address.

collections.title: Heroes & History
collections.essay: Carlyle's lifelong wager that history is the biography of great men — traced across his three monuments. The Calvinist formation of the prophet, his theory of revolution as a sea of fire, and the hero-worship of his vast Friedrich.

atlas._comment: The Atlas map: pin each corpus to a region id from atlas/geo.json (authored by scripts/build_atlas_geo.py). 'cell' = n/s/e/w/c (or an index) steers which slice a corpus gets when several share a region; 'z':'base' draws a nation under the state insets on top of it. 'grow' swaps a small region for a roomier one once >1 corpus lands there.

carlyle-french-revolution-research.label: Scotland — by the sage of Chelsea
